title: Ukrainian Almond Pudding
description: A traditional Ukrainian dessert featuring almond paste and a unique blend of flavors, served in a scallop shell. This dish is kosher-friendly and perfect for a cozy evening.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a blender or food processor, combine almond paste, milk, sugar, nuka-pickle brine, eggs, vanilla extract, and salt, and blend until smooth.
Strain the mixture into a bowl to remove any air bubbles.
Whisk in the melted butter until fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into scallop shells or small ramekins.
Place the shells on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake for 25 minutes or until the edges are set and the centers are still slightly jiggly.
Remove from the oven and let cool to room temperature.
Chill in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours before serving.
Refrigerate and consume within 1 week — this preparation is not shelf-stable without an approved acid level and canning process.

Refrigerator pickle only: store in the fridge and consume within 1 week. For shelf-stable canning, add at least 2 tablespoons of vinegar (5% acidity) per cup of brine and follow a tested water-bath process.
